findings.evidence: `rg -n "namespace AlphaP|pointsMulEquiv|pointsHom|mem_range_pointsHom_iff" code/TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/AdditiveFrobeniusKernel.lean` shows only `pointsHom`, `pointsHom_injective`, and `mem_range_pointsHom_iff`; adjacent examples expose `pointsMulEquiv` in `Trivial.lean:52`, `RootsOfUnity.lean:123`, and `SplitTorus.lean:68`.
findings.fix: Add a `pointsMulEquiv`/similar bundled `MulEquiv` from `WithConv (CoordinateRing p →ₐ[R] A)` to the subtype/subgroup `{a : Multiplicative A // (Multiplicative.toAdd a)^p = 0}`, with apply and inverse simp lemmas.
findings.issue: `AlphaP` identifies points only via `pointsHom`, `pointsHom_injective`, and `mem_range_pointsHom_iff`, leaving users to destruct a range existential instead of using a bundled equivalence with the p-nilpotent subgroup.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[simp\].*(mk_ι_pow_eq_zero)|theorem mk_ι_pow_eq_zero"` hits only `theorem mk_ι_pow_eq_zero` at line 186.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `mk_ι_pow_eq_zero` so the coordinate relation `(x̄)^p = 0` rewrites automatically.
findings.issue: `mk_ι_pow_eq_zero` is the generator relation for the quotient coordinate ring but is not marked as a normal-form simp lemma.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[simp\].*(mem_range_pointsHom_iff)|theorem mem_range_pointsHom_iff"` hits only `theorem mem_range_pointsHom_iff` at line 263; `toAdd_pointsHom` at line 237 is already marked `@[simp]`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `mem_range_pointsHom_iff` unless it is replaced by a bundled points equivalence whose apply/range lemmas carry the corresponding simp annotations.
findings.issue: `mem_range_pointsHom_iff` is the characteristic range predicate for the points map but lacks a simp tag, so membership in the exposed image does not normalize to p-nilpotence.

summary: The new public surface still stops at an injective map plus a range predicate, so downstream users do not get the promised points identification as a usable equivalence. Two characteristic normal-form lemmas are also missing the automation tags expected for this API.
